changes_summary: Revised from v1 to address all four gate flags. Dropped Step 3 (Layout.tsx changes) entirely — wrong layer since GenerationsPane is position:fixed. Simplified the fix from introducing calc-based height in two layers to a two-line change: (1) remove the paddingBottom override for video editor in LayoutMainContent, (2) change h-screen to h-full in VideoEditorPage. Removed all references to raw generationsPaneHeight as a fallback. Added two new regression tests specifically covering the video editor shell branch in LayoutMainContent.test.tsx.
questions: Is the 'compressed button below the video' the Maximize/Minimize timeline button in the full editor's divider bar, or a different UI element? This affects whether additional CompactPreview changes are needed.
Should the video editor compress when the generations pane is hovered (temporarily open) as well as when locked? The existing offset path handles both — this plan preserves that behavior.
Are there other panes (shots, tasks) that should also affect the video editor's available space, or is this only about the bottom generations pane?
success_criteria: Locking the generations pane while on `/tools/video-editor?timeline=...` causes the full editor to shrink vertically, with the timeline and preview compressing to fit above the generations pane.
Unlocking the generations pane restores the editor to full viewport height.
The compression/expansion transition is smooth (300ms ease-smooth).
The compact editor pane (top) continues to work correctly — no regression.
New regression tests in `LayoutMainContent.test.tsx` cover the video-editor-shell + locked-generations-pane branch.
No z-index or overlap issues between the video editor and the generations pane.
assumptions: The bug is about the full-screen video editor page (`/tools/video-editor?timeline=xxx`), not the compact editor pane. The compact pane already compresses via EditorPaneTab's generationsOffset subtraction.
The 'compressed button below the video' refers to a known UI reference behavior where the editor content shrinks to minimum space.

Only the bottom generations pane needs to affect the video editor height. Side panes (shots, tasks) already don't render their margin offsets when `isVideoEditorShellActive` — this plan does not change that.

`paddingBottom` on an `h-screen overflow-hidden` container correctly reduces the available content area for `h-full` children — verified by tracing the CSS box model: the padding is inside the 100vh box, shrinking the content area that `h-full` measures against.
